meta_description: Metal binder jet 3D printing is a technology used to make affordable metal parts with excellent mechanical properties. Get a quote on metal prototype and production parts.

sections.heading: Metal Binder Jetting 3D Printing Service
sections.text: Metal Binder Jetting 3D Printing Service Upload your CAD files to get an instant quote on parts. Free shipping on all US orders. See How It Works

sections.heading: Fast and Affordable Metal Parts
sections.text: Fast and Affordable Metal Parts Metal binder jetting (MBJ) is a 3D printing technology used to make complex metal parts with excellent mechanical properties. MBJ is often used to produce metal parts at a fraction of the cost of other processes, with fewer design limitations. Binder jetting machines have large build areas and produce parts at high speed, making the process an excellent option for low to medium-batch, rapid-turn parts. The speed and affordability of binder jetting make it ideal for industries that need high-strength parts, including industrial, automotive, consumer products, oil, and gas.

sections.heading: What is the Metal Binder Jetting Process
sections.text: What is the Metal Binder Jetting Process Metal binder jetting is an additive manufacturing metal printing technology that builds metal parts from a CAD file in a multi-step process. Binder jet machines first fuse layers of metal powder together with a binding agent. Once the part is bound, the resulting shape is called a green part and is left to cure. The cured form is then put into a furnace to sinter or be infiltrated with bronze.Â Since binder jet parts are supported by unused powder on all sides of the part, this eliminates or minimizes the need for post-processing. Since most binder jet parts must undergo a furnace stage, parts are prone to shrinkage, are slightly porous, and fine fea...

sections.heading: Surface Finish
sections.text: Surface Finish Our stainless steel 3D printed parts using metal binder jetting are post-processed with a standard "Zirblast" finish. This zirconia-based ceramic bead blast finish mildly peens the surface without significantly altering fine features or dimensions. The resulting appearance will exhibit a uniform, matte, light gray surface with a slightly satin-like appearance.

sections.heading: Binder Jetting Design Guidelines
sections.text: Binder Jetting Design Guidelines Feature Recommendation Feature Unsupported walls Recommendation 1.0 mm (0.04'') Feature Supported walls Recommendation 1.0 mm (0.04'') Feature Minimum feature size Recommendation 1.0 mm (0.04'') Feature Minimum hole diameter Recommendation 1.0 mm (0.04'') Feature Minimum escape hole diameter Recommendation 4.0 mm (0.157'') Feature Minimum font size Recommendation Arial 26 or greater To see more design-for-manufacturing recommendations, view our Binder Jetting Design Guide .

sections.heading: Best Applications for Metal Binder Jetting
sections.text: Best Applications for Metal Binder Jetting Rapid Tooling Because binder jet metal parts are strong and affordable, they are widely used for rapid tooling, fixtures, and jigs. Prototyping Binder jet metal 3D printing is affordable and fast, and therefore is often used to make rapid to fully functional prototypes. Mass Production Highly dense and durable metals make binder jet printing a fantastic choice for end-use products.
